recommendations: Continue enforcing all locked decisions and non-negotiable invariants strictly
Maintain discipline on milestone cadence updates and docs verification
Keep running all drift and calibration checks (vision, docs, legibility, standards, evaluator, adversarial, inference) before merges
Ensure all new behavior includes success and failure path tests
Regularly update and verify phase status metadata and evidence paths
Sustain nightly adversarial taxonomy runs and evaluator calibration freshness
Preserve repository legibility and team standards encoding with automated drift gates
Use inference drift reports to preemptively address potential drift before enforcement mode
Keep benchmark-to-production bridge contract and promotion gates unified and up to date
Continue to document and enforce run isolation and ephemeral observability guarantees

ruleHints.hint: Ensure the workflow system prompt is non-empty and includes explicit role, constraints, and verification posture.
resolutionSteps: Set a non-empty workflow.systemPrompt with clear instructions.
Include explicit role definitions, constraints, and verification intent in the prompt.
Reject deployment or execution if the system prompt is blank.

ruleHints.hint: Add explicit constraint language using MUST, NEVER, DO NOT, or ONLY to the workflow system prompt.
resolutionSteps: Incorporate non-negotiable guardrail language in the system prompt.
Document tool and data boundaries clearly within the prompt.

ruleHints.hint: Include verification and acceptance language such as PASS, FAIL, VERIFY, or ACCEPTANCE in the system prompt.
resolutionSteps: Define explicit verification expectations in the prompt.
Add PASS/FAIL acceptance criteria for workflow outputs.
Ensure verification language is clear and actionable.

ruleHints.hint: For commit tool nodes, ensure a matching propose phaseGroup node exists to enable preview and verification before commit.
resolutionSteps: Add a tool node with sideEffectMode='propose' and matching phaseGroup before commit nodes.
Keep commit nodes with sideEffectMode='commit' and the same phaseGroup.
Emit preview artifact hash prior to executing commit actions.

ruleHints.hint: Specify timeoutMs and retryLimit for each node to manage execution time and retry budgets.
resolutionSteps: Add timeoutMs and retryLimit properties to all nodes missing them.
Implement escalation to human operators after retry budget exhaustion.

ruleHints.hint: Define a valid memoryPolicy with retrievalMode, maxContextItems, writebackEnabled, and piiRetention settings.
resolutionSteps: Set memoryPolicy.maxContextItems to an integer between 1 and 200.
Explicitly configure memoryPolicy.writebackEnabled and piiRetention values.
Validate memoryPolicy against schema before deployment.

suggestions.attack: An attacker crafts user input that attempts to override or bypass system-level prompts or constraints by injecting instructions that negate or replace system directives.
suggestions.expectedDefense: Strict parsing and sanitization of user inputs to detect and block instructions that attempt to override system prompts; enforce immutable system prompt constraints at runtime.

suggestions.attack: A workflow node calls a tool with overly broad or wildcard permissions (e.g., '*' or 'resource:*'), allowing access to unauthorized resources or actions beyond intended scope.
suggestions.expectedDefense: Enforce strict permission scoping on all tool calls; disallow wildcard or unscoped permissions; require explicit least-privilege permissions for each tool invocation.

suggestions.attack: Workflow objective or system prompt lacks explicit denial of cross-tenant data access, allowing implicit or indirect access to data from other tenants.
suggestions.expectedDefense: Require explicit tenant boundary language denying cross-tenant access in workflow objectives and system prompts; implement runtime tenant data isolation checks.

suggestions.attack: An attacker injects malicious or misleading data into the workflow memory context, causing corrupted or poisoned memory state that influences future workflow decisions.
suggestions.expectedDefense: Implement strict validation and sanitization of all memory writes; use integrity checks and anomaly detection on memory context; isolate memory per tenant and workflow.

suggestions.attack: User input embeds nested instructions or encoded commands that evade simple keyword detection and attempt to manipulate the prompt or workflow behavior.
suggestions.expectedDefense: Use advanced NLP techniques to detect nested or encoded instructions; apply multi-layered input validation and context-aware filtering.

suggestions.attack: An attacker chains multiple tool calls in a workflow to escalate permissions indirectly, exploiting intermediate tools with broader permissions.
suggestions.expectedDefense: Analyze tool call chains for cumulative permission escalation; enforce permission checks at each step; limit chaining of tools with sensitive permissions.

suggestions.attack: Data from one tenant is leaked to another tenant via shared resources or improperly isolated caches or memory segments.
suggestions.expectedDefense: Enforce strict tenant isolation on all shared resources; implement tenant-aware caching and memory segmentation; audit data flows for cross-tenant leaks.

suggestions.attack: Misconfiguration of memory policies allows retention of personally identifiable information (PII) beyond allowed limits, risking data poisoning and privacy violations.
suggestions.expectedDefense: Validate memory policy configurations to enforce PII retention rules; audit memory writes for PII content; apply automatic redaction or expiration of sensitive data.
